seo_description: Current time in Bratislava, Slovakia. Live CET/CEST clock — UTC+1 standard, UTC+2 summer. Bratislava time vs Vienna, Prague, London, New York.

faq.question: What time is it in Bratislava right now?
faq.answer: Bratislava operates on Central European Time (CET/UTC+1) during winter and Central European Summer Time (CEST/UTC+2) during summer. Slovakia observes EU Daylight Saving Time, advancing clocks one hour in late March and setting them back in late October. Use the live clock above to see the exact current time in Bratislava.

faq.question: What time zone is Bratislava in?
faq.answer: Bratislava is in the Central European Time zone (CET), which is UTC+1 during standard time (late October to late March) and CEST/UTC+2 during summer (late March to late October). This is the same timezone as Vienna, Prague, Budapest, Warsaw, Berlin, and Paris — making Bratislava well aligned with its major Central European neighbours.

faq.question: Does Bratislava observe Daylight Saving Time?
faq.answer: Yes. Bratislava and all of Slovakia follow EU Daylight Saving Time rules. Clocks advance 1 hour forward on the last Sunday in March at 2:00 AM CET (becoming 3:00 AM CEST/UTC+2), and fall back 1 hour on the last Sunday in October at 3:00 AM CEST (becoming 2:00 AM CET/UTC+1). This is synchronised across the European Union.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Bratislava and London?
faq.answer: Bratislava is 1 hour ahead of London during winter (CET vs GMT) and 1 hour ahead during summer (CEST vs BST), since both the UK and Slovakia observe DST on the same schedule. The gap remains a constant 1 hour year-round. When it is 10:00 AM in London, it is 11:00 AM in Bratislava.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Bratislava and Vienna?
faq.answer: There is no time difference between Bratislava and Vienna. Both cities use Central European Time (CET/UTC+1 in winter, CEST/UTC+2 in summer) and observe EU DST on exactly the same schedule. The two capitals are just 60 km apart, making Bratislava one of the closest capital city pairs in the world.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Bratislava and New York?
faq.answer: Bratislava is 6 hours ahead of New York during winter (CET/UTC+1 vs EST/UTC-5) and 6 hours ahead during summer (CEST/UTC+2 vs EDT/UTC-4), since both regions observe DST on similar schedules. The gap is typically 6 hours year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 3:00 PM in Bratislava.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Bratislava from the US?
faq.answer: From New York (EST), the best window to reach Bratislava during business hours (9:00 AM – 5:00 PM CET) is between 3:00 AM – 11:00 AM Eastern. Most practical are early morning Eastern calls — 7:00 AM New York corresponds to 1:00 PM Bratislava. From the West Coast (PST/UTC-8), the gap is 9 hours, so morning calls at 7:00 AM Pacific reach Bratislava at 4:00 PM.

faq.question: What are business hours in Bratislava?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Bratislava run 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM or 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM CET/CEST, Monday through Friday. Government offices and banks are open 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM. Bratislava's Old Town retail shops typically operate 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM weekdays and 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM on Saturdays. The city's expanding fintech and IT sectors follow flexible hours aligned with Western European business norms.

content_blocks.title: Bratislava Time Zone — CET/CEST, EU Daylight Saving
content_blocks.content: Bratislava, the capital of Slovakia and the smallest capital city by area in the European Union, operates on Central European Time (CET/UTC+1) in winter and Central European Summer Time (CEST/UTC+2) in summer. Slovakia follows the standard EU DST schedule: clocks advance on the last Sunday in March and fall back on the last Sunday in October, in lockstep with Austria, Germany, France, and all other CET-zone EU members. Bratislava is uniquely positioned as one of only two world capital cities directly bordering another capital — Vienna is just 60 km away across the Austrian border, sharing the exact same timezone and DST schedule. The city is also 1 hour ahead of London year-round (both observe DST together), 6 hours ahead of New York, and 7 hours behind Tokyo. Its Central European position makes it well-suited for business coordination across Europe.

content_blocks.title: Bratislava — Central European Business Hub and Fintech Growth
content_blocks.content: Bratislava sits on the Danube River at Slovakia's southwestern corner, sharing borders with Austria and Hungary. The city has grown rapidly into a significant Central European business hub, particularly in fintech, IT outsourcing, shared services, and automotive manufacturing. Major financial institutions and tech companies have established regional offices in Bratislava, attracted by lower operating costs compared to Vienna or Prague while remaining in the EU single market with the euro as currency. Business culture aligns closely with Western European norms: weekday office hours, professional meetings in English or German, and strong digital infrastructure. The Bratislava Stock Exchange operates in CET. Slovakia joined the EU in 2004 and the eurozone in 2009, making Bratislava a fully integrated part of the European financial system.

content_blocks.title: Scheduling Meetings with Bratislava Internationally
content_blocks.content: Bratislava's CET/CEST timezone (UTC+1 in winter, UTC+2 in summer) makes it easy to coordinate with most European cities. The 1-hour advantage over London means morning London meetings align with mid-morning Bratislava. With New York (typically 6 hours behind), the practical collaboration window is 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM Bratislava time, corresponding to 3:00 AM – 6:00 AM Eastern — making afternoon New York calls the pragmatic choice for real-time discussion. With Dubai (UTC+4), Bratislava is 3 hours behind in winter and 2 hours behind in summer, allowing solid morning overlap. With Singapore or Tokyo, the gap of 7–8 hours means video calls are best scheduled early morning Bratislava time or late afternoon Singapore time. Slovakia's country code is +421, and Bratislava's city code is 2.

time_difference_table.difference: 3 hours behind Dubai (2 hours in summer)
9 hours ahead (8 hours when LA is on PDT)
7 hours behind Tokyo (8 hours in winter)

timezone_facts.full_name: Central European Time / Central European Summer Time
timezone_facts.dst_start: Last Sunday in March at 2:00 AM CET
timezone_facts.dst_end: Last Sunday in October at 3:00 AM CEST
